---
title: "smic_core_microbiome"
output: html_document
date: "2026-03-13"
---



```{r }

decontam_filter_nonegs <- readRDS("C:/Users/Carolyn/Documents/seastar-microbiome-paper/code/decontam_filter_nonegs.rds")

```



```{r}
library(microbiome); packageVersion("microbiome")
library(RColorBrewer)
library(reshape)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)

```


Subset by sea star species
```{r}

healthy_derm  <- ps_filter(
  decontam_filter_nonegs,
  (Host == "Dermasterias imbricata" & DermisType2 == "H_healthy"))


healthy_pisaster  <- ps_filter(
  decontam_filter_nonegs,
  (Host == "Pisaster ochraceus" & DermisType2 == "H_healthy"))

seawater  <- ps_filter(
  decontam_filter_nonegs,
  (Host == "Seawater"))

```


##### CORE MICROBIOME ANALYSIS FOR EACH HOST/ENVIRONMENT #####

```{r core microbiome PISASTER HEALTHY}

#first need to transform the data
healthy_pisaster_trans <- microbiome::transform(healthy_pisaster, "compositional")

# Agglomerate first -- since we have repeated Genera in the taxa table
pisaster_healthy_genus <- tax_glom(healthy_pisaster_trans, "Genus")

# Melt phyloseq object to data frame
df_core_pisaster <- psmelt(pisaster_healthy_genus)

# Detect core genera 
core_genus_pisaster_healthy <- core_members(pisaster_healthy_genus, detection = 0.001, prevalence = 75/100)

#map back to taxonomic names rather than ASV Ids
core_taxa_pioc <- tax_table(pisaster_healthy_genus)[core_genus_pisaster_healthy, ]

core_tax_df_pioc <- as.data.frame(core_taxa_pioc)

pioc_taxa_list<-core_tax_df_pioc$Genus

pioc_taxa_list


#### Option 1: Add a Core column to the melted data frame ####
# Add a new column to df_core_pisaster data frame to indicate the core genera or other
df_core_pisaster <- df_core_pisaster %>% mutate(Core = ifelse(OTU %in% core_genus_pisaster_healthy, Genus, "Other")) 

df_core_pisaster

#set plotting order
unique(df_core_pisaster$Core)
df_core_pisaster$Core = factor(df_core_pisaster$Core, levels = c("Spirochaetia Class","Spirochaetaceae Family", "Salinispira", "Rhodobacteraceae Family", "Yoonia-Loktanella", "Flavobacteriaceae Family", "Polaribacter", "Saprospiraceae Family", "Endozoicomonas","Reichenbachiella","Roseibacillus","Rubritalea","Other" ))                        

 #write.csv(df_core_pisaster, "df_core_pisaster.csv")

# Plot everything

#df_core_pisaster$Date <- as.Date(df_core_pisaster$Date, format="%Y-%m-%d")
df_core_pisaster$YearMonth<-factor(df_core_pisaster$YearMonth)

core_colors <- c(
  "Spirochaetia Class" = "#8B004B",
  "Spirochaetaceae Family" = "#B2182B",
  "Salinispira" = "#EF6548",
  "Rhodobacteraceae Family" = "#F4A261",
  "Yoonia-Loktanella" = "#E9C46A",
  "Flavobacteriaceae Family" = "#F1E4A6",
  "Polaribacter" = "#D9E4A5",
  "Saprospiraceae Family" = "#B8DE8F",
  "Endozoicomonas" = "#7FCDBB",
  "Reichenbachiella" = "#41B6C4",
  "Roseibacillus" = "#1D91C0",
  "Rubritalea" = "#6A51A3",
  "Other" = "grey80",
  "NA" = "grey40"
)

df_core_pisaster$Core
pis_core_plot<-df_core_pisaster %>%
ggplot(aes(SiteType, Abundance, fill = Core)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "fill") +
  theme_classic() +
  scale_fill_manual(values = core_colors, drop = FALSE) +
  #facet_wrap(~SiteType) +
  #theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 30, hjust = 1)) + 
  ggtitle("P. ochraceous") + theme(plot.title = element_text(size = 10, face = "italic"))

pis_core_plot
```

#now derm
```{r}

#first need to transform the data
healthy_derm_trans <- microbiome::transform(healthy_derm, "compositional")

# Agglomerate first -- since we have repeated Genera in the taxa table
derm_healthy_genus <- tax_glom(healthy_derm_trans, "Genus")

# Melt phyloseq object to data frame
df_core_derm <- psmelt(derm_healthy_genus)

# Detect core genera 
core_genus_derm <- core_members(derm_healthy_genus, detection = 0.001, prevalence = 75/100)

#map back to taxonomic names rather than ASV Ids
core_taxa_derm <- tax_table(derm_healthy_genus)[core_genus_derm, ]

core_tax_df <- as.data.frame(core_taxa_derm)

derm_taxa_list<-core_tax_df$Genus

derm_taxa_list

#### Option 1: Add a Core column to the melted data frame ####
# Add a new column to df_core_pisaster data frame to indicate the core genera or other
df_core_derm <- df_core_derm %>% mutate(Core = ifelse(OTU %in% core_genus_derm, Genus, "Other")) 


#set plotting order
unique(df_core_derm$Core)

df_core_derm$Core = factor(df_core_derm$Core, levels = c("Saprospiraceae Family","Rubritalea", "Flavobacteriaceae Family", "Rhodobacteraceae Family","Other" ))                        

# write.csv(df_core_pisaster, "df_core_pisaster.csv")

# Plot everything

df_core_derm$YearMonth<-factor(df_core_derm$YearMonth)

derm_core_plot<-df_core_derm %>%
ggplot(aes(SiteType, Abundance, fill = Core)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "fill") +
  theme_classic()+
  scale_fill_manual(values = core_colors, drop = FALSE) +
 # facet_wrap(~SiteType) +
 # theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 30, hjust = 1)) + 
  ggtitle("D. imbricata") + theme(plot.title = element_text(size = 10, face = "italic"))

derm_core_plot


#to make sure colors of core taxa are consistent when two species are combined
core_levels <- c(
  "Spirochaetia Class",
  "Spirochaetaceae Family",
  "Salinispira",
  "Rhodobacteraceae Family",
  "Yoonia-Loktanella",
  "Flavobacteriaceae Family",
  "Polaribacter",
  "Saprospiraceae Family",
  "Endozoicomonas",
  "Reichenbachiella",
  "Roseibacillus",
  "Rubritalea",
  "Other",
  "NA"
)


df_core_derm$Core <- factor(df_core_derm$Core, levels = core_levels)
df_core_pisaster$Core <- factor(df_core_pisaster$Core, levels = core_levels)

library(ggpubr)
ggarrange(pis_core_plot, derm_core_plot, ncol=1, common.legend=TRUE, align = "v", legend = "right", labels = c("a", "b"))


```


And seawater core taxa, out of interest

```{r}
#first need to transform the data
seawater_trans <- microbiome::transform(seawater, "compositional")

# Agglomerate first -- since we have repeated Genera in the taxa table
seawater_genus <- tax_glom(seawater_trans, "Genus")

# Melt phyloseq object to data frame
df_core_seawater <- psmelt(seawater_genus)

# Detect core genera 
core_genus_seawater <- core_members(seawater_genus, detection = 0.001, prevalence = 75/100)

#map back to taxonomic names rather than ASV Ids
core_taxa_seawater <- tax_table(seawater_genus)[core_genus_seawater, ]

core_tax_df_seawater <- as.data.frame(core_taxa_seawater)


seawater_taxa_list<-core_tax_df_seawater$Genus

seawater_taxa_list

#### Option 1: Add a Core column to the melted data frame ####
# Add a new column to df_core_pisaster data frame to indicate the core genera or other
df_core_seawater <- df_core_seawater %>% mutate(Core = ifelse(OTU %in% core_genus_seawater, Genus, "Other")) 


#set plotting order
unique(df_core_seawater$Core)
```


If we wanted to do a venn diagram to compare the core taxa between sea stars

```{r}


library(ggVennDiagram)

venn <- list(
  Derm = derm_taxa_list,
  Pioc = pioc_taxa_list, 
  Seawater = seawater_taxa_list)

ggVennDiagram(venn, label = "count") +
  scale_fill_gradient(low = "white", high = "steelblue")


Derm <- unique(derm_taxa_list)
Pioc <- unique(pioc_taxa_list)
Seawater <- unique(seawater_taxa_list)

derm_only <- setdiff(Derm, union(Pioc, Seawater))
pioc_only <- setdiff(Pioc, union(Derm, Seawater))
seawater_only <- setdiff(Seawater, union(Derm, Pioc))

derm_pioc <- setdiff(intersect(Derm, Pioc), Seawater)
derm_seawater <- setdiff(intersect(Derm, Seawater), Pioc)
pioc_seawater <- setdiff(intersect(Pioc, Seawater), Derm)

all_three <- Reduce(intersect, list(Derm, Pioc, Seawater))

all_three

```
